subject: Re: warp_tools 2D Particle Series and Relion5: reconstruct vs initial model
body:
Hi Philipp,
Great question! 
We’re actually already not totally compliant with R5 so even if the tilt series were correct tomo_reconstruct_particle there wouldn’t work.
Could you elaborate on the pose changing from TM? You just mean that if you run initial model in C1 it can come out different?
The machinery is there for reconstructing from 2D… Dimitry and I have discussed adding a reconstruction routine but there is nothing there yet. The best advice right now would probably be to do a 3D particle export then a relion_reconstruct
Hope that helps!
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Aug 8, 2024, at 14:33, 3P1L <philipp.stawski@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿
Dear All,
This question not only concerns warp_tools but more generally 2D particle series and WARP + relion 5, but I hope somebody has a suggestion for me 😇
In our particular case, we are running relion 5.0-beta-0-commit-84ba16 and warp_tools on linux.
We use a python version of TOMOMAN to export TS to warp_tools and pytom_GPU for template matching.
We then extract the particle series in warp_tools.
When I generate an initial model from the 2D particle series, everything looks fine and I can run a refinement afterwards. [a bit annoying that the particle pose can change relative to the TM reference, but OK ... 😉]
However, having to run an initial model just to check if particle picks / particles are good is a bit cumbersome ...
From the traditional (3d subtomo) workflow, I would just use relion_reconstruct to get the initial reference.
However, when trying to use relion_tomo_reconstruct_particle on my optimization set (the initial one from warp_tools), I only get empty volumes.
I suppose this is bc warp_tools will only link a "dummy_tiltseries.mrc"?
Is there a way to actually link the correct data in a relion-v5 approved way?
Are there any other options in v5 + warp_tools to reconstruct the particle series directly (i.e. without alignment)? [as relion reconstruct does not work on 2D particle stacks]
Thanks!




--
